title: US Measles Cases Reach Highest Level in Decades
subtitle: New data shows 2026 is the worst year for measles in the United States since 1991 as vaccination rates continue to fall.

summary.en: The United States has officially recorded more measles cases in 2026 than in any year since 1991. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) reported 2,318 cases, surpassing the previous record set in 2025. Health experts warn that the country may lose its status as a measles-free nation due to low vaccination rates. The American Academy of Pediatrics has called the situation an avoidable crisis that harms children. Officials worry that the current public health system is not prepared to stop the spread of this disease.
summary.zh: 美國二〇二六年正式記錄的麻疹病例數，已創下一九九一年以來的新高。疾病管制暨預防中心通報了二千三百一十八例，超越了二〇二五年創下的紀錄。衛生專家警告，由於疫苗接種率偏低，該國可能失去麻疹絕跡國家的地位。美國兒科學會稱此為一場傷害兒童且本可避免的危機。官員們擔憂目前的公共衛生體系尚未準備好阻止此疾病的擴散。

paragraphs.en: The United States has officially reached a worrying new milestone in 2026. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), the country has now recorded 2,318 cases of measles. This number is higher than the 2,289 cases reported during all of 2025. Because there are still five months left in the year, health officials fear that the final total will be much higher. This is the highest number of cases the country has seen since 1991.
paragraphs.zh: 美國在二〇二六年正式達到了一個令人擔憂的新里程碑。根據疾病管制暨預防中心（CDC）的數據，該國目前已記錄到二千三百一十八例麻疹病例。此數字已超過二〇二五年全年通報的二千二百八十九例。由於今年還剩下五個月，衛生官員擔心最終總數將會高出許多。這是該國自一九九一年以來出現的最高病例數。

paragraphs.en: Measles is a highly contagious disease that was declared eliminated in the United States in 2000. For many years, the country kept the disease under control by ensuring that most people received vaccines — injections that help the body fight off specific illnesses. However, recent data from the CDC shows that vaccination rates have dropped. To stop the spread of measles, about 95% of the population needs to be vaccinated. Currently, the national rate has fallen below this level, making it easier for the virus to move through communities.
paragraphs.zh: 麻疹是一種傳染性極強的疾病，曾於二〇〇〇年被宣告在美國絕跡。多年來，該國透過確保大多數民眾接種疫苗（即協助身體對抗特定疾病的注射劑），成功將此病控制住。然而，疾病管制暨預防中心近期的數據顯示，疫苗接種率已經下降。為了阻止麻疹擴散，約需百分之九十五的人口完成接種。目前，全國接種率已跌破此水準，使得病毒更容易在社區間傳播。

paragraphs.en: Large outbreaks have already affected many people in states such as South Carolina, Utah, and Arizona. Other smaller clusters of the disease have appeared in several additional states. Because of these rising numbers, international health officials are planning to meet in November. At that time, they will decide if the United States and Mexico should lose their official status as measles-free countries. If they lose this status, it would mean that measles is once again a regularly circulating disease in the region.
paragraphs.zh: 大規模的疫情爆發已影響南卡羅來納州、猶他州與亞利桑那州等地的許多民眾。其他幾個州也出現了較小規模的群聚感染。由於病例數不斷攀升，國際衛生官員計畫於十一月召開會議。屆時，他們將決定美國與墨西哥是否應失去「麻疹絕跡國家」的官方地位。若失去此地位，意味著麻疹已再次成為該地區定期傳播的疾病。

paragraphs.en: Experts warn that the actual number of people who have been sick is likely much higher than the official count. According to The Guardian, researchers believe that many infected people never go to a doctor or a hospital, so their cases are never recorded. This makes it difficult for public health officials to know the true scope of the outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 專家警告，實際染病人數很可能遠高於官方統計數字。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，研究人員認為許多受感染者從未就醫或前往醫院，因此他們的病例從未被記錄在案。這使得公共衛生官員難以掌握疫情的真實規模。

paragraphs.en: The American Academy of Pediatrics has expressed deep concern about these findings. In a recent statement, the group described the record-breaking numbers as an “avoidable crisis that disproportionally hurts children.” They noted that these cases represent real people, including babies who have been hospitalized with pneumonia, students who have missed weeks of school, and parents who have had to stop working to care for their sick children. The group stated that the country should not accept this situation as a new normal.
paragraphs.zh: 美國兒科學會（American Academy of Pediatrics）對這些調查結果表達了深切擔憂。該組織在近期的一份聲明中，將這些破紀錄的數字描述為一場「本可避免，且對兒童造成不成比例傷害的危機」。他們指出，這些病例代表的是真實的人，包括因肺炎住院的嬰兒、被迫缺課數週的學生，以及必須停止工作以照顧生病子女的家長。該組織表示，國家不應將此現狀視為新常態。

paragraphs.en: Improving the situation will be difficult. Many experts say that the country needs a nationwide effort to increase vaccination rates. However, this appears unlikely under the current leadership. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the US health secretary, has a long history of downplaying the importance of immunizations and often speaks about their risks. His approach has been met with sharp criticism from medical professionals who believe his views make it harder to protect public health.
paragraphs.zh: 改善現況將十分困難。許多專家認為，國家需要採取全國性的行動來提升疫苗接種率。然而，在現任領導層下，這似乎不太可能實現。美國衛生部長小羅伯特·甘迺迪（Robert F. Kennedy Jr.）長期以來一直淡化疫苗接種的重要性，並經常談論其風險。他的作法遭到醫學專業人士的強烈批評，他們認為其觀點使得保護公共衛生變得更加困難。

paragraphs.en: This measles outbreak is happening at the same time that the United States is dealing with several other health threats. These include bird flu, whooping cough, and a record-breaking outbreak of a parasite called Cyclospora, which has affected thousands of people across 41 states. These events have highlighted growing concerns about the nation’s ability to prevent and stop infectious diseases. Former top CDC officials have warned that the public health system has been weakened by cuts to staffing and funding. They argue that the country is currently in a weaker position to handle these health challenges than it was before the Covid-19 pandemic.
paragraphs.zh: 此次麻疹疫情爆發的同時，美國也正應對其他幾項健康威脅。其中包括禽流感、百日咳，以及一種稱為圓孢子蟲（Cyclospora）的寄生蟲所引發的破紀錄疫情，該疫情已影響四十一州數以千計的民眾。這些事件凸顯了外界對於國家預防與遏制傳染病能力的日益擔憂。疾病管制暨預防中心的前高層官員警告，公共衛生體系已因人力與資金削減而遭到削弱。他們主張，與新冠肺炎疫情前相比，該國目前處理這些健康挑戰的能力更為薄弱。

paragraphs.en: As the situation continues to develop, the focus remains on whether the government can change its approach. Without a major shift in policy and a renewed focus on vaccination, experts worry that America’s children will continue to pay the price for this public health crisis. For now, the country remains in a difficult position, struggling to contain a disease that was once considered a thing of the past.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢持續發展，焦點仍集中在政府是否能改變其應對方式。若政策沒有重大轉變，且未能重新聚焦於疫苗接種，專家擔心美國的兒童將持續為這場公共衛生危機付出代價。目前，該國仍處於困境之中，努力遏制一種曾被視為已成過去式的疾病。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To defend oneself against or successfully resist an illness.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語由動詞與介系詞組成，意思非字面拆解可得。文中：injections that help the body fight off specific illnesses.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make something seem less important or serious than it really is.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 輕描淡寫、貶低……的重要性。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞常在新聞中描述對危機的態度，容易被誤解為單純的「向下」。文中：Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the US health secretary, has a long history of downplaying the importance of immunizations and often speaks about their risks.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To experience the negative consequences of an action or a situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 付出代價（指承擔負面後果）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此慣用語並非指金錢交易，而是指承擔錯誤決策的後果。文中：experts worry that America’s children will continue to pay the price for this public health crisis.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: US surpasses 2025’s record number of measles infections as cases climb

previousArticles.title: U.S. Faces Record-Breaking Parasite Outbreak
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States is currently experiencing a record-breaking outbreak of a parasite called Cyclospora. Health agencies have reported over 11,500 total cases across 41 states. Officials are investigating links between the illness and contaminated lettuce from Mexico. Experts warn that budget cuts and staff shortages are slowing down the response. Despite the high number of cases, officials remain divided on the severity of the situation.

previousArticles.title: Record-Breaking Parasite Outbreak Continues as Officials Search for Answers
previousArticles.summaryEn: A record-breaking outbreak of a parasite called cyclospora is spreading across the United States. Health officials report that cases have now passed 6,100 in Michigan alone. The FDA is investigating a link to lettuce from Taylor Farms despite recent testing errors. Experts note that tracing such diseases is difficult because they are hard to identify in small samples. For now, officials recommend that consumers wash their hands and food surfaces carefully to prevent infection.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the significance of the 95% vaccination rate mentioned by the CDC?

options.A: It is the percentage of the population that has already been vaccinated in the US.
options.B: It is the threshold required to effectively prevent the transmission of measles.
options.C: It is the target set by the US health secretary to combat the current outbreak.
options.D: It is the number of people who have recovered from measles in 2026.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確指出「To stop the spread of measles, about 95% of the population needs to be vaccinated.」，這代表 95% 是阻止病毒傳播的必要門檻。選項 A 錯誤，因為文中提到目前接種率已低於此水平；選項 C 錯誤，文中並未將此目標歸功於衛生部長；選項 D 則完全未提及。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the potential impact of the upcoming November meeting of international health officials?

options.A: The US government will likely implement a mandatory vaccination policy for all citizens.
options.B: The meeting will result in the immediate eradication of measles in North America.
options.C: The US might lose its international recognition as a country that has successfully eliminated measles.
options.D: The meeting will focus primarily on funding for the Cyclospora parasite outbreak.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段，國際衛生官員將在十一月開會決定美國與墨西哥是否應失去「無麻疹國家」的官方地位，這暗示了該國在公共衛生控制上的退步。選項 A 無法由文中推論；選項 B 與現實情況矛盾；選項 D 雖提及其他傳染病，但該會議主題明確是針對麻疹地位。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'avoidable crisis' imply about the measles outbreak?

options.A: The outbreak was caused by an unpredictable natural disaster.
options.B: The outbreak could have been prevented through proper public health measures.
options.C: The outbreak is a minor issue that will resolve itself without intervention.
options.D: The outbreak is a result of a new, untreatable strain of the virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中，「avoidable」意指「可避免的」，結合上下文提到接種疫苗率下降是主因，說明若能維持足夠的疫苗接種，這場危機本是可以預防的。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 輕視了問題的嚴重性；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提到病毒株變異，而是強調疫苗接種率下降。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the current measles situation in the US?

options.A: The US public health system is performing better than it did during the Covid-19 pandemic.
options.B: Measles is a minor illness that does not require significant government attention.
options.C: The resurgence of measles highlights systemic failures in vaccination and public health management.
options.D: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is the sole individual responsible for the current health crisis.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文整體論述了麻疹病例激增的現象，並歸因於疫苗接種率下降、公共衛生系統弱化以及政策領導方向的疑慮，強調這是一個嚴重的公共衛生挑戰。選項 A 與第七段內容矛盾；選項 B 忽略了文章對危機的警告；選項 D 雖然提到其觀點有爭議，但未將其視為唯一責任人，過度簡化了問題。
